questions.text: A figure has ______ symmetry if it can be folded so that the two halves match exactly.
questions.explanation: Line symmetry is also called reflection symmetry.

questions.text: How many lines of symmetry does a regular triangle (equilateral) have?
questions.explanation: It has 3 lines of symmetry passing through vertices and midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.text: A square has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Two diagonals and two lines joining mid-points of opposite sides.

questions.text: A circle has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Every diameter is a line of symmetry.

questions.text: The angle of rotational symmetry for a square is:
questions.explanation: 360 / 4 = 90. Rotating a square by 90° looks identical.

questions.text: The order of rotational symmetry for an equilateral triangle is:
questions.explanation: It fits onto itself 3 times in a full turn.

questions.text: Which of the following letters has a vertical line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Letter 'A' can be divided vertically down the middle.

questions.text: Which of the following letters has rotational symmetry but no line symmetry?
questions.explanation: 'S' looks the same after 180° rotation but cannot be folded along a line.

questions.text: An isosceles triangle has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Only one line through the vertex between equal sides.

questions.text: A scalene triangle has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: No sides or angles are equal, so no line can split it evenly.

questions.text: The order of rotational symmetry for a circle is:
questions.explanation: A circle looks the same after rotation by any angle.

questions.text: A rectangle has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Two lines connecting mid-points of opposite sides.

questions.text: Does a rectangle have rotational symmetry? If yes, of what order?
questions.explanation: It looks identical after 180° and 360° rotation.

questions.text: Which capital letter of English alphabet has both horizontal and vertical line symmetry?
questions.explanation: H can be folded both ways. (O, X, I also work).

questions.text: A regular hexagon has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: For any regular polygon, number of lines of symmetry = number of sides.

questions.text: The fixed point around which a figure is rotated is called:
questions.explanation: Basic definition in rotational symmetry.

questions.text: Rotation turns an object through an angle. A half-turn corresponds to:
A rhombus has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: The two diagonals are the only lines of symmetry.

questions.text: Order of rotational symmetry for a rhombus is:
questions.explanation: It looks identical after 180° and 360°.

questions.text: A parallelogram (general) has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Folding does not result in matching halves unless it's a special case like rectangle.

questions.text: Order of rotational symmetry for a parallelogram is:
questions.explanation: Rotation of 180° brings it back to original appearance.

questions.text: A line of symmetry is also known as a:
questions.explanation: Folding along it produces a mirror image.

questions.text: Every object has rotational symmetry of at least order ______.
questions.explanation: Every object looks the same after a full 360° turn.

questions.text: Angle of rotation for order 3 is:
Which shape has exactly one line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Only the line joining midpoints of parallel sides (if sides are equal).

questions.text: Does the letter 'Z' have line symmetry?
questions.explanation: Folding it doesn't match halves, but it has rotational symmetry.

questions.text: Rotational symmetry order of 'Z' is:
questions.explanation: Identical after 180°.

questions.text: For a regular pentagon, angle of rotation is:
Which quadrilateral has both line and rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: All three have at least order 2 rotational and 2 line symmetries.

questions.text: Centre of rotation for a circle is its:
questions.explanation: Basic property of a circle.

questions.text: Line symmetry is related to ______.
questions.explanation: Line symmetry is mirror reflection.

questions.text: Number of lines of symmetry in a regular n-gon is:
questions.explanation: Standard rule for regular polygons.

questions.text: A fan with 3 blades has rotational symmetry of order:
questions.explanation: Symmetry depends on the number of identical blades.

questions.text: Which digit has both horizontal and vertical line symmetry?
questions.explanation: Depending on font, these digits often show dual symmetry.

questions.text: Rotational symmetry of order 4 means the angle of rotation is:
Does an equilateral triangle have rotational symmetry of order 3?
questions.explanation: Standard property.

questions.text: Symmetry is important in:
questions.explanation: Symmetry creates balance and aesthetic appeal.

questions.text: If a shape has line symmetry, must it have rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: Letter 'A' has line symmetry but no rotational symmetry (other than order 1).

questions.text: If a shape has rotational symmetry, must it have line symmetry?
questions.explanation: Letter 'S' has rotational symmetry but no line symmetry.

questions.text: The line dividing a figure into two identical parts is the:
questions.explanation: General term used in this chapter.
